abstract_ts: This paper proposes a wireless single-phase induction motor (SPIM) system driven by two half-cycle sinusoidal currents in stator windings separately, enabling a simplified and robust receiver-side structure without using active switch components. A decoupled two-channel wireless power and drive transfer (WPDT) system with orthogonal coils is designed to readily power and drive the SPIM. Moreover, the influence of winding's time constant on current waveform deformation is newly revealed and identified to determine the optimal parameters of the stator windings. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed wireless SPIM system is verified by simulation results. The dynamic performance of the SPIM, including acceleration and torque output, is validated through finite element analysis. The system demonstrates significant potential in various applications like humanoid and joint robots, and pipeline transportation.

abstract_ts: This research seeks to utilize machine learning methods to forecast the behavior of recycled concrete, specifically emphasizing the evaluation of its compressive strength. By gathering and manipulating recycled concrete performance data, we identified significant factors such as water-cement ratio, aggregate replacement rate, and porosity index to support the model with reliable input. In the model selection stage, we compared multiple machine learning algorithms and finally selected support vector machine as the optimal model. The model performed excellently on the test set, especially showing good prediction capabilities on evaluation indicators such as mean square error and mean absolute error. The findings demonstrate that machine learning techniques offer substantial benefits in understanding the intricate nonlinear connections in recycled concrete, offering fresh insights for the optimal utilization of recycled materials in design and engineering. This research confirms that machine learning is effective in materials science and shows its high potential in sustainable materials research. Experimental design and cross-validation guarantee the reliability and repeatability of results, offering a new method to evaluate the performance of recycled concrete with practical significance.

abstract_ts: The fast acceptance of cloud computing has exacerbated the intricacy and vulnerability of network infrastructures, giving them attractive targets for modern assaults. Routine intrusion detection systems (IDS) typically insufficiently satisfy the real-time needs of dynamic cloud environments. This project develops and evaluates AI-driven intrusion detection systems (AI-IDS) that use machine learning models to detect and respond to attacks in real time. Deep learning and band methods improve anomaly detection accuracy and decrease false positives in the proposed system. Supervised and unsupervised learning were used to enhance detection rates and flexibility. Assessment of open to the public datasets and factual cloud settings indicates tremendous gains in threat detection and system robustness. This study underlines the capabilities of AI-driven solutions in securing cloud networks from evolving cyber threats.

abstract_ts: The increasing need for efficient spectrum management arises from the speed of sending 5G networks into service to support different applications while keeping peak network performance levels. Spectrum management tactics which work traditionally do not have the capability to calibrate efficiently to the complex 5G environment hence encountering the efficiency problems of distributing spectrum resource. This research assesses cognitive fuzzy logic algorithms as revolutionary methods for 5G networks high spectrum management structures. The cognitive fuzzy logic combines fuzzy logic reasoning with cognitive computing technology to provide smart real time decision system at dynamic operational situations. The proposed framework leverages user demand along with traffic pattern with interference level and QoS to make optimal spectrum allocation decision. The fusion of fuzzy systems structure and cognitive models results in an algorithm that offers superior spectral performance and reduced latency times with guaranteed fair distribution of resources. Simulation tests along with performance analyses ensure successful verification that the proposed method provides outstanding results as compared to conventional spectrum management practices. The research shows that cognitive fuzzy logic is a transformational solution to spectrum challenges in 5G networks and support the intelligent sustainable wireless communication.

abstract_ts: Most traits in animal breeding, including feed efficiency traits in pigs, are affected by many genes with small effect and have a moderately high heritability between 0.1 and 0.5, which enables efficient selection. Since the microbiota composition in the gastrointestinal tract is also partly heritable and was shown to have a substantial effect on feed efficiency, the host genes affect the phenotype not only directly by altering metabolic pathways, but also indirectly by changing the microbiota composition. The effect m i  of the microbiota composition on the breeding value g i  of an animal i is the conditional expectation of its breeding value, given the vector φ i  with microbiota frequencies, that is  m i = E  g i | φ i   .  The breeding value g i  of an animal can therefore be decomposed into a heritable contribution m i  that arises from an altered microbiota composition and a heritable contribution p i  that arises from altered metabolic pathways within the animal, so  g i = m i + p i .  Instead of selecting for breeding value  g ^ i  , an index comprising the two components  m ^ i  and  p ^ i  with appropriate weights, that is  I i = λ 1 m ^ i + λ 2 p ^ i   , can be used. The present study shows how this breeding strategy can be applied in pig genomic selection breeding scheme for two feed efficiency traits and daily gain.

abstract_ts: Myopic Macular Degeneration (MMD) is a visionthreatening complication of pathological myopia. Timely identification and categorisation are crucial for efficient management of MMD. It is important to stress the need for early and accurate diagnosis of Myopic Macular Degeneration (MMD) through automated and non-invasive computational methods. This work demonstrates a thorough methodology for the automated identification of Myopic Macular Degeneration (MMD) by feature extraction utilising discrete wavelet transform (DWT) for conventional machine learning classifiers. Our work involves extracting features from fundus images and utilizing them for the categorization of Myopic Macular Degeneration (MMD), provides an alternative to conventional diagnostic methods that typically rely on biometric parameters such as choroidal thickness, axial length, and related anatomical measurements The developed framework aids ophthalmologists in detecting subtle retinal texture variations and classifying MMD with improved clinical relevance and diagnostic reliability. Different established machine learning algorithms were employed for classification tasks. Logistic Regression and Gradient Boosting emerged as the most robust classifiers and Daubechies-4, db4 yielding the most distinct separation between normal and myopic classes.

abstract_ts: This study presents a multilingual sentiment analysis framework for evaluating public sentiments on India's National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. The authors developed a dataset related to NEP 2020 using web scraping from open sources. The curated dataset comprises 50,000 social media posts (English: 30,000, Hindi: 12,000, Tamil: 8,000) processed through a confidence-gated hybrid annotation pipeline. Sentiment labels were created using Transformer models (BERT, mBERT, XLMR) and validated by native-speaker with F1-scores of 87.6%, 81.2% and 78.0% for English, Hindi and Tamil respectively: outperforming baselines (SVM, Naive Bayes, BiLSTM) by 12-18% (p<0.001). We use computational efficiency measures to illustrate that training takes 3.2-5.3 hours and inference lasts between 118 and 187 posts per second. Topic modeling revealed sentiment divergences: positive for linguistic inclusivity and teacher training, negative for affordability and infrastructure. Cross-linguistic analysis showed English-Hindi convergence (similarity: 0.61) versus Tamil divergence (0.46), reflecting regional priorities. Tamil emphasized linguistic identity while English prioritized implementation critiques. Quantitative policy impact analysis shows very strong correlation (r=0.68, p<0.01) between regional sentiment scores and state adoption rates. This open-sourced contribution is filling the crucial gap of inclusive policy analytics in multilingual society informing evidence-based policy.

abstract_ts: Edge Computing has emerged as a key paradigm for enabling latency-sensitive applications for mobile users. A central challenge in edge computing service provisioning is service placement, which must balance user proximity, resource fairness, and execution efficiency. Existing approaches to service placement include heuristics-based ones, exact optimization approaches and those that use metaheuristics. In general, trade-offs emerge where either optimal placement is possible, but is feasible only at small scale, or scalability is offered at the expense of load balance and reliability. In this paper, we propose a hybrid ILP-SA algorithm for service placement at the edge that combines the exploratory power of Simulated Annealing (SA) with the exactness of Integer Linear Programming (ILP). The hybrid algorithm operates in two stages: SA first generates diverse feasible candidates, then ILP refines them to ensure optimality within a reduced search space. This design achieves both scalability and solution quality. We implement and evaluate the approach in Simu5G across MEC networks with 10-200 hosts, comparing against classical heuristics, ILP, SA, CP, GA, and ACO. Results show that ILP-SA achieves balanced resource utilization, competitive service-toedge server distances, and low execution latency (1 ms), while outperforming CP, GA, and SA in scalability.

abstract_ts: Digital signal processing involves recording a signal, making changes to it, and generating a new signal. In engineering, procedures have been developed to acquire signals and extract useful data from them. Once the characteristics have been extracted, they are classified using statistical and artificial intelligence methods. Understanding, comparing and using these characteristics is an important step before tackling massive data sets and deep learning algorithms. The limited capabilities of the embedded cards that can be included in EMG prostheses must also be considered. This paper proposes a basic exploratory analysis and the use of classical algorithms to decompose a set of EMG data. Various machine learning classification algorithms were evaluated, including multilayer perceptron neural networks, support vector machines (SVM), K-nearest neighbors (KNN), decision trees and random forests. The results were then compared using the accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and support value metrics. Classic machine learning algorithms are still an option for programming embedded systems that operate based on EMG signals. The quality and cleanliness of the data are as important as the choice of AI model.

abstract_ts: Distributed systems are easier to build than ever with the emergence of new, data-centric abstractions for storing and computing over massive datasets. However, similar abstractions do not exist for storing and accessing meta-data. To fill this gap, Tango provides developers with the abstraction of a replicated, in-memory data structure (such as a map or a tree) backed by a shared log. Tango objects are easy to build and use, replicating state via simple append and read operations on the shared log instead of complex distributed protocols; in the process, they obtain properties such as linearizability, persistence and high availability from the shared log. Tango also leverages the shared log to enable fast transactions across different objects, allowing applications to partition state across machines and scale to the limits of the underlying log without sacrificing consistency.

abstract_ts: Accurate calibration of force-torque sensors is essential for enabling safe and responsive physical interactions between humans or objects and humanoid robots. Traditional calibration approaches struggle to capture nonlinearities, to find a suitable cross-coupling modeling, and to deal with sensor drift, limiting their reliability in real-world applications. This paper presents a hybrid calibration framework that combines deep feedforward neural networks with a residual correction stage using Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost). The proposed approach was applied to calibrate four force-torque sensors of six degrees of freedom, that will be integrated in the mobile platform of a wheeled humanoid platform. Extensive tuning and testing were carried out for each sensor, evaluating different layer configurations and hyperparameters. The final models achieved high accuracy on both training and testing data. Across all sensors, the XGBoost residual learner improved the overall prediction performance measured by the root mean square error (RMSE), especially for the full-dataset. Additionally, inference time evaluations confirmed the suitability of the proposed method for forces and torques estimation in real-time implementation. The results support the hybrid model's robustness and adaptability in multi-axial force sensing calibration for robotic interaction tasks.

abstract_ts: Computation offloading could improve performance, energy efficiency, and resource utilization by transferring computation tasks from a local device to a remote server or cloud. With the rise of the Internet of Things (IoT) and latency-sensitive applications (AR/VR, autonomous driving, etc.), cloud-edge offloading has become a crucial paradigm. This paper presents a survey of recent cloud-edge offloading research. We classify state-of-the-art offloading techniques from heuristic algorithms and fuzzy logic to game-theoretic methods and deep reinforcement learning, and link them to application domains. We analyze the quality objectives these techniques optimize (e.g., latency, energy, throughput, fairness) and detail the evaluation methodologies used. We specify current technological challenges, outline best practices for verification, and propose standardized evaluation frameworks. The paper concludes with open challenges (such as improving reproducibility, standardizing benchmarks, and enhancing industry adoption) and highlights promising research directions to advance a more robust and practical cloud-edge offloading ecosystem.

abstract_ts: Nonstabilizerness is a fundamental resource for quantum advantage, as it quantifies the extent to which a quantum state diverges from those states that can be efficiently simulated on a classical computer, the stabilizer states. The stabilizer Rényi entropy (SRE) is one of the most investigated measures of nonstabilizerness because of its computational properties and suitability for experimental measurements on quantum processors. Because computing the SRE for arbitrary quantum states is a computationally hard problem, we propose a supervised machine-learning approach to estimate it. In this work, we frame SRE estimation as a regression task and train a Random Forest Regressor and a Support Vector Regressor (SVR) on a comprehensive dataset, including both unstructured random quantum circuits and structured circuits derived from the physics-motivated one-dimensional transverse Ising model (TIM). We compare the machine-learning models using two different quantum circuit representations: one based on classical shadows and the other on circuit-level features. Furthermore, we assess the generalization capabilities of the models on out-of-distribution instances. Experimental results show that an SVR trained on circuit-level features achieves the best overall performance. On the random circuits dataset, our approach converges to accurate SRE estimations, but struggles to generalize out of distribution. In contrast, it generalizes well on the structured TIM dataset, even to deeper and larger circuits. In line with previous work, our experiments suggest that machine learning offers a viable path for efficient nonstabilizerness estimation.

abstract_ts: Our proposed work intends to talk about the advantages of online hiring and selection for businesses. The study demonstrates how the organization can profit from online recruiting and selection by identifying the best candidate for the position. This research uses metadata for content analysis with an emphasis on the advantages of online hiring. The current developments in the hiring and selection process are quite surprising. Although the paper's main goal is to show how much time and money may be saved, the features Several internet job boards are also briefly covered to provide a comprehensive summary of the recently established system for hiring. The study used social media as an example, outlining the benefits and drawbacks of the current hiring process. However, this study addresses some of the disadvantages of online recruiting and selection. For businesses trying to access a larger candidate pool and expedite the hiring process, online recruiting and selection can be a useful tool. To guarantee the greatest outcomes, it is critical to be conscious of the possible disadvantages and take action to lessen them. The study examined the benefits of an online hiring and selection procedure using metaanalysis. The proposed portal leverages blockchain for secure storage and verification of user credentials, ensuring authenticity and eliminating the need for third party verification. Smart contracts automate processes such as job applications, offer letters, and payments, reducing delays and administrative overhead. Furthermore, the system enhances user privacy by providing individuals authority over their information, enabling them to share specific information with employers on a need-to-know basis.

abstract_ts: Networks require IDS systems for their cybersecurity protection to defend against unauthorized access and both malicious activities and cyberattacks. Traditional IDS systems face difficulties when detecting sophisticated and evolving threats because of increasing cyberattack complexity. The research focuses on combining Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and deep learning methods to boost cyber security intrusion detection functionality. The synthetic dataproducing mechanism of Generative Adversarial Networks utilizes generator-discriminator models which produce realistic intrusion pattern simulations for better detection model training. Through deep learning methodology patterns analysis the convolutional neural networks (CNNs) along with recurrent neural networks (RNNs) deliver reliable solutions for anomaly detection and classification. This proposed framework resolves issues associated with unbalanced datasets as well as it provides real-time identification capabilities and responds dynamically to changing cybersecurity threats. Public cybersecurity datasets reveal how GAN-augmented deep learning systems perform superior to classic IDS regarding zero-day attack detection while decreasing false alarm rates. This research introduces GANs with deep learning as an innovative intrusion detection solutions which delivers scalable, productive and proactive cybersecurity solutions. The work develops for intelligent IDS more advanced capabilities as well as establishes foundational framework for future automated IDS.

abstract_ts: While it has become increasingly common for researchers, practitioners and policymakers to draw from the insights of former extremists to combat violent extremism, overlooked in this evolving space has been an in-depth look at how formers perceive such efforts. To address this gap, interviews were conducted with 10 Canadian former right-wing extremists based on a series of questions provided by 30 Canadian law enforcement officials and 10 community activists. Overall, formers suggest that combating violent extremism requires a multidimensional response, largely consisting of support from parents and families, teachers and educators, law enforcement officials, and other credible formers.

abstract_ts: This study employs data from both kindergarten cohorts of the Early Childhood Longitudinal Study (n ~ 12,450 in 1998; n ~ 11,000 in 2010) to assess whether associations between preschool participation and children's academic and behavioral outcomes-both at school entry (Mage  = 5.6 years in both cohorts) and through third grade-have changed over time. Findings are strikingly similar across these two, nationally representative, U.S. cohorts: preschool is positively associated with academic outcomes and negatively associated with behavioral outcomes both at school entry and as children progress through school. Heterogeneity is documented with respect to child and preschool characteristics. However, there is no evidence that associations between preschool and medium-term child outcomes differ by elementary school characteristics.

abstract_ts: Latinx-Americans are underserved across healthcare contexts, and racial disparities in pain management are pervasive. One potential contributor is racial bias in pain perception - including low-level implicit biases and explicitly held lay-beliefs. Delays in seeking pain treatment may compound these disparities. However, experiments testing these factors in the context of Latinx-American pain are limited, and mechanisms by which Latinx-American group-membership influences pain perception and treatment are not understood. Here, Latinx-American and White-American participants read vignettes including a Latinx or White patient's pain description and numerical pain rating. Participants then rated how much pain they thought each patient was in using the same numerical scale. Participants also reported how much pain they themselves would need to experience to prompt treatment-seeking. In contrast to prior work identifying lay beliefs that Latinx-Americans feel less pain than White-Americans, participants in the current study revealed a bias in the opposite direction. This was largely driven, however, by Latinx-American participants, who have been under-represented in previous studies of empathy and pain perception. Latinx-Americans ascribed more pain to patients overall - irrespective of patient race - relative to White-Americans. Latinx-American participants also reported that their own pain would need to be significantly more intense before seeing a doctor. These results suggest that, relative to White-Americans, Latinx-Americans may be more likely to believe people are in more pain than they report - or may be more perceptive of others' pain - and that they may be in more pain upon presenting to medical settings.

abstract_ts: Host-microbiota interaction plays fundamental roles in the homeostasis of mucosal immunity. Dysbiosis of intestinal microbiota has been demonstrated to participate in various immune responses and many multifactorial diseases. Study of intestinal microbiota has moved beyond the consequences of dysbiosis to the causal microbiota associated with diseases. However, studies of pulmonary microbiota and its dysbiosis are still in their infancy. Improvement of culture-dependent and -independent techniques has facilitated our understanding of lung microbiota that not only exists in healthy lung tissue but also exerts great impact on immune responses under both physiological and pathological conditions. In this review, we summarize recent progresses of lung microbiota dysbiosis and its impact on the local immune system that determines the balance of tolerance and inflammation. We discuss the causal roles of pulmonary dysbiosis under disease settings, and propose that the interaction between lung microbiota and host is critical for establishing the immune homeostasis in lung.

abstract_ts: A green, simple, sensitive, efficient and rapid method was developed based on application of a novel deep eutectic solvent in vortex-assisted dispersive liquid-liquid microextraction (VA-DES-DLLME) of two auxins (indole-3-acetic acid (IAA) and 1-naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA)) in water and fruit juice samples followed by high performance liquid chromatography determination. The novel hydrophobic deep eutectic solvent was readily prepared by mixing isoamyl alcohol as hydrogen bonding donor and trioctylmethylammonium chloride as hydrogen bonding acceptor at room temperature for 3 min. Affecting parameters on VA-DES-DLLME were investigated and optimized. Under the optimum conditions, good linearity (1.0–500 μg L−1, r2 ≥ 0.99) was obtained for the auxins. Limit of detection (LOD) was 0.2 and 0.3 μg L−1 for IAA and NAA, respectively. Moreover, repeatability (intra-day, n = 6) and reproducibility (inter-day, n = 3) of the method expressed as relative standard deviation (RSD) at 10 and 100 μg L−1 concentration levels were <8%. Finally, applicability of the developed method was evaluated by analyzing the auxins in water and fruit juice samples and satisfactory results were obtained (recovery ≥ 90%)..

abstract_ts: Whereas the diagnosis of moderate and severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is readily visible on current medical imaging paradigms (magnetic resonance imaging [MRI] and computed tomography [CT] scanning), a far greater challenge is associated with the diagnosis and subsequent management of mild TBI (mTBI), especially concussion which, by definition, is characterized by a normal CT. To investigate whether the integrity of the blood-brain barrier (BBB) is altered in a high-risk population for concussions, we studied professional mixed martial arts (MMA) fighters and adolescent rugby players. Additionally, we performed the linear regression between the BBB disruption defined by increased gadolinium contrast extravasation on dynamic contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging (DCE-MRI) on MRI and multiple biomechanical parameters indicating the severity of impacts recorded using instrumented mouthguards in professional MMA fighters. MMA fighters were examined pre-fight for a baseline and again within 120 h post-competitive fight, whereas rugby players were examined pre-season and again post-season or post-match in a subset of cases. DCE-MRI, serological analysis of BBB biomarkers, and an analysis of instrumented mouthguard data, was performed. Here, we provide pilot data that demonstrate disruption of the BBB in both professional MMA fighters and rugby players, dependent on the level of exposure. Our data suggest that biomechanical forces in professional MMA and adolescent rugby can lead to BBB disruption. These changes on imaging may serve as a biomarker of exposure of the brain to repetitive subconcussive forces and mTBI.

abstract_ts: Hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) has been established as an effective therapy for selected inborn errors of metabolism. The success of HSCT in metabolic disease is best exemplified through the treatment of Hurler's syndrome, a lysosomal storage disease. Through the collaborative effort of several international centers, factors that predict successful patient and transplant outcomes have been identified. In this review, we discuss the principles that underlie the use of HSCT in metabolic diseases. We consider the clinical indications, conditioning regimens, and disease-specific follow-up for HSCT in different metabolic diseases. We highlight persisting challenges in HSCT to delay progression of certain organ systems that remain refractory to HSCT and the relatively high rates of aplastic graft failure. Finally, we evaluate the variable applicability of these principles to other inherited metabolic disorders including peroxisomal, mitochondrial, and other lysosomal storage diseases.

abstract_ts: Environment perception technology is the foundation of intelligent vehicles and an indispensable core function of automatic driving. In this work, we propose a multi-sensor fusion perception confidence enhancement algorithm for the object perception confidence fluctuation problem in complex scenes. In our method, evidence theory is used for the calculation of fusion confidence, making full use of the large pixel density of images with rich information and high classification confidence, and the high accuracy of 3-dimensional spatial localization of LiDAR. It improves the quality of object perception in complex environments, and provides more reliable perception support for safe navigation and autonomous decision-making in the un-manned domain. We conducted object perception fusion experiments on the proposed algorithm, and the fusion algorithm is able to perceive the objects in the surrounding environment well and fuse the perception results.

abstract_ts: Education is essential for the development of emerging countries, fostering economic growth, social stability, and technological advancement. Competitive robotics provides a practical framework for students to apply and acquire technical knowledge through real-world challenges. This paper presents a case study on the development of Rockbot, a low-cost teleoperated MiniSumo robot, as a Project-Based Learning initiative at Pontificia Universidad Católica del Perú. Rockbot served as a reference for students to design and implement their own competition robots. Survey data revealed that 87.2% of participants reported academic and technical improvement, particularly in areas such as mechanical design, electronics, and programming.

abstract_ts: This paper proposes a new reliability and security model of aviation communication network, and analyzes its performance by experimental simulation. First, a reliability evaluation model based on graph theory and Markov chain is designed, and the fault tolerance and recovery ability of the network are improved by combining redundancy mechanism and recovery strategy; secondly, a comprehensive security protection algorithm is proposed through game theory and intrusion detection technology to deal with various network attack threats. The experiment results indicate that the recovery time of the system can be reduced by 23.5% on average, and the system is able to prevent 95.3% DDoS attacks and guarantee the security of data transfer. Finally, it is proved that the reliability and safety model presented in this paper can significantly improve the overall performance of aviation communication network, especially in the case of complicated failure and attack. Compared with the traditional method, the performance of the system is improved by 17.8%• The research provides a new way to optimize the reliability and safety of aviation communication network, and provides the basis for the application and development of future network techniques.

abstract_ts: This paper focuses on achieving the most efficient use of land environmental policies using sensor networks and adaptive systems to optimise the management of urban regions with rapid growth. The work highlights how data collected by sensor networks can guide and adjust policy based on real-time data, making land-use decisions more sustainable. Through an entire sensor network over a highly urban area, the paper evaluates the effects of adaptive systems on water consumption, erosion, air quality, and soil quality. The findings show a significant decrease in water use, land degradation, and air and soil quality in the locations where the system was deployed. These results show how adaptive systems and sensor networks can make land management better, and provide policymakers with a novel way to respond to environmental and socioeconomic pressures in rapidly evolving urban areas. Not only does the proposed model ensure better policy responsiveness, it also supports sustainable urban development with the support of real-time data and dynamic policy changes.

abstract_ts: High volumes, short buffer times and many other players influence the maritime transport chain and intraport container transport. Congestion at logistics hubs leads to inefficient processes, increased waiting times, costs and emissions. Truck appointment systems are designed to reduce congestion by offering trucking companies time slots for delivering or collecting containers. However, these systems lack flexibility, which limits utilization and reduces efficiency. The study aims to develop strategies to evaluate and improve these systems in a simulation study. The results show that medium-length time slots improve the situation for transparent processes, or long time slots for limited transparency. Overbooking and open access are mutually exclusive, but offer slight advantages individually. Adjustments to opening hours of other nodes show little benefit. Overall, flexibility options positively influence the ratio of load trips and waiting times.

abstract_ts: Extracting Agent-Based Models (ABMs) from data, also known as Data-Driven Agent-Based Modeling (DDABM), requires a clear understanding of data requirements and their mappings to the corresponding ABM components. DDABM is a relatively new and emerging topic, and as such, there are only highly customized and problem-specific solutions and approaches. In our previous work, we presented a framework for DDABM, identifying the different components of ABMs that can be extracted from data. Building on this, the present study provides a comprehensive analysis of existing DDABM approaches, examining prevailing trends and methodologies, focusing on the mappings between data and ABM components. By synthesizing and comparing different DDABM approaches, we establish explicit mappings that clarify data requirements and their role in enabling DDABM. Our findings enhance the understanding of DDABM and highlight the role of data in automating model extraction, highlighting its potential for advancing data-driven agent-based simulations.

abstract_ts: High-fidelity simulation models of variable frequency drives often incur expensive computation due to high granularity, complex physics and highly stiff components, hindering real-time Digital Twin Industry 4.0 applications. Surrogate models can outperform simulation solvers by orders of magnitude, potentially making real-time virtual drives feasible within practical computational limits. Despite this potential, current surrogate models suffer from limited generalizability and robustness. In this paper, we present an industrial case study exploring the combination of deep learning with surrogate modeling for simulating variable frequency drives, specifically replacing the induction motor high-fidelity component. We investigate the performance of Long-Short Term Memory-based surrogates, examining how their prediction accuracy and training time vary with synthetic datasets of different sizes, and how well the induction motor surrogates generalize across different motor resistances. This initial study aims to establish a foundation for further development, benchmarking and automation of surrogate modeling workflow for simulation enhancement.

abstract_ts: Industrial configuration planning requires testing many setups, which is time-consuming when each scenario must be evaluated through detailed simulation. To accelerate this process, we train a multi-layer perceptron to predict key performance indicators quickly, using it as a surrogate model. However, classical regression metrics such as mean squared error, mean absolute error, and root mean squared error do not reflect prediction quality in all situations. To solve this issue, we introduce a classification-based evaluation strategy. We define acceptable prediction margins based on business constraints, then convert the regression output into discrete classes. We assess model performance using precision and recall. This approach reveals where the model makes critical errors and helps decision-makers at airbus helicopters trust the artificial intelligence predictions.

abstract_ts: Psiguajdianone (1), a novel caryophyllene-derived meroterpenoid dimer, was isolated from Psidium guajava. The structure of 1 was determined by X-ray analysis and confirmed by total synthesis. Our synthetic strategy involves biomimetic cascade Knoevenagel condensation/hetero-Diels-Alder reaction and dimerization. Notably, the caryophyllene-derived meroterpenoids obtained during our synthesis were first identified as artifacts in the laboratory, and five of them were proven to be natural products present in the plant. Moreover, these compounds show significant anti-inflammatory activity.

abstract_ts: This paper proposes a dynamic impact model of ESG ratings on financing based on reinforcement learning, aiming to optimize corporate financing decisions through deep reinforcement learning methods. This paper adopts the DQN algorithm, uses multiple variables such as ESG ratings, market risks, and corporate financial status as state inputs, and financing decisions (such as financing amount, financing interest rate, etc.) as actions. The optimization goal is to reduce financing costs and risks. The experimental results show that with the improvement of ESG ratings, the financing costs of enterprises show a significant downward trend, and the reinforcement learning model is more flexible than the traditional fixed rule financing model to adapt to changes in the market environment and effectively reduce financing costs. Specific data experiments show that when the ESG rating of an enterprise increases by 10%, its financing cost can be reduced by about 3.2%, and the prediction accuracy of the model is as high as 94%. In addition, in the process of optimizing financing decisions, the model can also flexibly adjust strategies and improve financing efficiency, which is crucial to the enhanced ability of reinforcement learning in dealing with complex decision-making problems. The research in this paper not only provides an innovative financing optimization method for the financial field, but also provides new ideas for the application of ESG ratings in financing, and promotes the process of transformation in the financial field.

abstract_ts: The simplified finite element model of the temperature gradient distribution of the UHV DC bushing was established by using the finite element analysis software Ansoft. The temperature field simulation analysis was performed and converted into the influence of resistivity, and the trend of the influence of temperature distribution on resistance in the insulating medium was obtained. By loading the resistivity of the changed insulating material in the electric field finite element analysis model, the potential and electric field distribution of the bushing considering the influence of temperature gradient under different working conditions can be calculated. The results show that the electric field intensity is the largest in the DC voltage field strength distribution of the sleeve under the temperature gradient at the junction of the flange and the sleeve shed. The maximum field strength is 2.27 mV/m, which is about 9.67% higher than the maximum field strength under the DC voltage without considering the influence of temperature. The field strength of the outer surface of the high-voltage bushing at the flange gradually decreases along the shed. Because the temperature of the silicone rubber from the center guide rod to the outside is gradient distributed, the SF6 temperature is higher and the silicone rubber temperature close to the air side is lower, because the resistivity of the insulating medium has the characteristics of negative temperature and the field strength distribution is proportional to the resistivity.

abstract_ts: This article proposes a new decision tree algorithm for corporate financial risk prediction, overcoming not only the deficiencies of classic approaches but further improving the accuracy and computational efficiency of the model. This paper presents a complete framework by combining optimized feature selection mechanisms, novel pruning strategies, and ensemble techniques to reinforce the performance of prediction in complex financial situations. Experimental verification on a dataset of 5,000 corporate records for the 2018–2023 period, in comparison to traditional approaches, the new algorithm produced a remarkable enhancement of 93% for prediction accuracy and a 35% reduction of computational overhead. When tested across different market conditions, the proposed model returned an appreciable stability with a low standard deviation of 0.015 during cross-validation tests. The main novelties are a modified information gain ratio with incorporated financial domain knowledge, cost-compatibility pruning, and a weighted ensemble voting scheme that cumulatively contribute toward model robustness without compromising interpretability. These research contributions will bridge the theoretical development and real-world practices in corporate financial risk prediction with an effective and scalable solution to risk management in modern business contexts. It also indicates the way forward for future research, particularly in addressing class imbalance problems and refining the potentials of real-time processing of high-frequency financial data streams.

abstract_ts: This paper proposes an innovative intelligent analysis model, CNN-ClusterNet, which combines CNN and image clustering algorithm to improve the performance of data middle station in processing unstructured data. It can extract deep features from image data and get detailed information from it based on CNN's strong ability of image recognition; while the image clustering algorithm clusters these features to mine potential patterns and trends in the data. The model can automatically group and classify image data, helping the data middle station to achieve more accurate intelligent analysis. Experimental results show that CNN-ClusterNet improves the accuracy of image recognition by 4.3% compared with traditional methods, and the improvement in clustering accuracy is 12.1%. In addition, the real-time processing capability of the model has also been significantly improved, and the overall computing speed has been increased by 30%. This study shows that by organically combining CNN with image clustering algorithm, the ability of data middle station in unstructured data processing can be effectively improved, providing more accurate support for data-driven intelligent decision-making.

abstract_ts: The healthcare sector faces rising demands from aging populations, complex patient needs, and workforce shortages, requiring innovative solutions for resource and service coordination. In response, Trondheim municipality and St. Olavs hospital are developing integrated planning tools within the Research Council of Norway funded HARMONI project. This transdisciplinary initiative aims to bridge the widening gap between service demand and capacity through planning tools that offer an overview of patient flows and resource capacities. The tools will support comprehensive planning, process adjustments, and capacity dimensioning, fostering collaboration to prevent patient queues and unnecessary transfers. We present three ongoing hybrid model case studies focusing on patient flow between primary and specialist health services. These cases provide opportunities to develop hybrid simulation verification and validation techniques.

abstract_ts: Global cerebral ischemia (GCI) commonly occurs in the elderly. Subcortical white matter lesions and oligodendrocyte (OLG) loss caused by cerebral ischemia have been implicated in the development of post-ischemic depression and cognitive impairment. OLGs are necessary for axonal myelination; the disrupted differentiation of OLG progenitor cells (OPCs) is associated with impaired remyelination. Evidence has indicated that increased levels of inflammatory cytokines released from activated microglia induce depression-like behaviors by affecting neurotransmitter pathways, but the mechanisms remain elusive. We explored the potential mechanisms that link microglia activation with GCI-induced depression and cognitive dysfunction by studying effects of minocycline on white matter damage, cytokine levels, and the monoaminergic neurotransmitters. An acute GCI animal model was generated through bilateral common carotid artery occlusion to induce ischemic inflammation and subcortical white matter damage. Minocycline, an inhibitor of microglia activation, was intraperitoneally administrated immediately after surgery and continued daily for additional six days. Minocycline shortened the immobile duration in tail suspension test and forced swimming test, while no improvement was found in Morris water maze test. The plasma levels of IL-1β, IL-6, TNF-α, HMGB1, and netrin-1 were significantly reduced with the treatment of minocycline. Minocycline treatment substantially reversed demyelination in corpus callosum and hippocampus, alleviated hippocampal microglia activation, and promoted OPCs maturation, while no effect was found on hippocampal neurodegeneration. Besides, the content of dopamine (DA) in the hippocampus was upregulated by minocycline treatment after GCI. Collectively, our data demonstrated that minocycline exerts an anti-depressant effect by inhibiting microglia activation, promoting OPCs maturation and remyelination. Increased DA in hippocampus may also play a role in ameliorating depressive behavior with minocycline treatment.

abstract_ts: Adolescents with gender dysphoria (GD) often face various associated social, emotional, and behavioral difficulties. In such a marginalized group, it is crucial to identify factors that may impact psychological functioning to better accommodate their needs. Therefore, the present study investigated the impact of two specific risk factors, poor peer relations and general family functioning, on the development of psychological problems in adolescents with GD, and their possible interaction effect. The Youth Self-Report, a Peer Relations Scale, and a General Family Functioning scale were assessed in a sample of n = 180 clinically referred adolescents (mean age 15.5; 146 transgender boys with a female birth-assigned sex, and 34 transgender girls with a male birth-assigned sex) with a complete GD diagnosis (fulfillment of the DSM 5 criteria A and B) at their initial admission to the Hamburg Gender Identity Service. Multiple linear regression analysis was conducted to examine the relationship between peer relations, family functioning, and psychological functioning outcomes. Adolescents with GD presented significantly higher Internalizing and Total Problem scores compared to the German reference norm. Externalizing problems were above the norm for transgender boys, but within the normal range for transgender girls. Multiple regression analysis revealed that, overall, adolescents with an advanced age, a female birth-assigned sex, poorer peer relations, and poorer family functioning showed more behavioral and emotional problems. Consequently, incorporating both the family and social environment in transgender care is of high importance to adequately tend to the needs of adolescents with GD.

abstract_ts: Conventional chemotherapy for cancer treatment is usually compromised by shortcomings such as insufficient therapeutic outcome and undesired side effects. The past decade has witnessed the rapid development of combination therapy by integrating chemotherapy with hyperthermia for enhanced therapeutic efficacy. Near-infrared (NIR) light-mediated photothermal therapy, which has advantages such as great capacity of heat ablation and minimally invasive manner, has emerged as a powerful approach for cancer treatment. A variety of nanomaterials absorbing NIR light to generate heat have been developed to simultaneously act as carriers for chemotherapeutic drugs, contributing as heat trigger for drug release and/or inducing hyperthermia for synergistic effects. This review aims to summarize the recent development of advanced nanomaterials in chemo-photothermal combination therapy, including metal-, carbon-based nanomaterials and particularly organic nanomaterials. The potential challenges and perspectives for the future development of nanomaterials-based chemo-photothermal therapy were also discussed.

abstract_ts: Diabetes mellitus is a frequent cause of transtibial amputation, requiring precise fitting of the prosthesis to avoid ulcers and complications. The lack of diagnostic tools to identify critical pressures increases clinical risk and costs. In countries such as Peru, the price of a silicone prosthetic liner can exceed US$300, making it difficult to acquire. Faced with this reality, a wireless sensorized system capable of detecting pressure points effectively and affordably is proposed. The system acts as direct interface between the residual limb and the prosthesis, simulating real conditions of use. It uses FSR 402 sensors encapsulated in vacuum-cured medical silicone, connected to an ESP32 microcontroller that transmits data via WiFi to an interface developed in MATLAB. The platform allows visualizing pressures on a 3D model of the liner, identifying risk zones and recording data in CSV files. The results validate the technical feasibility of the prototype. The use of an operational amplifier increased the signal similarity to an ideal step by <tex>$58 \%$</tex>, improving stability. The silicon coated sensors showed a reduction in pressure values compared to the sensors without encapsulation. Even so, both cases presented a good fit to the logarithmic model, with low RMSE errors (24.20 with silicone and 25.25 without silicone) preserving a stable behavior. In addition, the average latency was <tex>$101.8 \pm 6.0 \text{ms}$</tex>, below the human perceptible threshold, allowing a real-time response. This system represents a practical, economical and adaptable solution for the clinical follow-up of patients with transtibial amputation due to diabetes.

abstract_ts: This paper presents the design and scaled validation of a modular embedded system for temperature control in broiler poultry houses. The proposed architecture includes a thermal prototype equipped with a ceramic heater, a ventilation fan, and a motorized curtain driven by a stepper motor. Control logic is executed on an ESP32 development board, while temperature sensing and transmission are handled by a RAK3172 module. A Python-based SCADA interface enables real-time monitoring and manual override. Three control strategies were considered: ON/OFF (implemented and experimentally validated), and PID and fuzzy logic (evaluated through simulation using identified plant models). Simulation results indicate that fuzzy logic controllers provide smooth actuator responses and minimal steady-state error, showing promising improvements in transient response compared to ON/OFF and PID. Experimental tests with ON/OFF control confirmed reliable operation and demonstrated the feasibility of low-cost embedded solutions for poultry environments. The modular design also allows future integration of advanced controllers with minimal code changes.

abstract_ts: In retrieval-augmented natural language processing systems, how information is segmented—or chunked-plays a critical role in determining the accuracy, efficiency, and robustness of downstream retrieval performance. Despite the increasing reliance on retrieval-based architectures such as open-domain question answering and RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), there remains limited empirical work comparing different chunking strategies in a systematic, task-specific manner. In this paper, we present a comprehensive comparative study of widely used chunking methods—fixed-size chunking, sentence-based chunking, recursive chunking and semantic similarity-based chunking—evaluated within the context of retrieval performance across diverse document types. We analyze each strategy across multiple metrics including Precision, Recall, MRR(Mean Reciprocal Rank), and chunking efficiency, emphasizing how performance varies significantly with the structural and semantic characteristics of different document types. Our findings high-light key trade-offs between retrieval accuracy and chunking granularity, revealing that while finer-grained semantic chunking often improves precision, it may also introduce computational overhead, especially in certain document types. These insights provide practical guidelines for developers and researchers building retrieval-centric NLP pipelines and lay the groundwork for future work on adaptive and task-aware chunking mechanisms.

abstract_ts: Bridge cracks are a critical defect that compromises the safety of transportation infrastructure. Traditional manual inspection methods face challenges in efficiency, subjective judgments, and the significant risks of working at heights. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes an automated method for bridge crack identification and quantitative analysis utilizing an improved YOLOv7 model and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The approach integrates high-resolution UAV-based imaging with deep learning, incorporating key optimizations in three areas: (1) the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to enhance feature extraction of fine cracks; (2) the adoption of a focal loss function and an adaptive data augmentation strategy to improve generalization in complex environments; and (3) the design of a lightweight network architecture to enable real-time processing on embedded UAV platforms. Experimental results on a dataset of 500 bridge crack images demonstrate that the improved model achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP@0.5) of 98.7%. The average measurement errors for crack length and width are 1.5 mm and 0.3 mm, respectively. The proposed method shows high precision and efficient for bridge structural health monitoring with considerable practical value for engineering applications.

abstract_ts: This paper designs a novel movement control system for a rebar tying robot to address issues in the construction industry arising from manual rebar tying, such as high labor intensity, significant safety hazards, and severe labor turnover. The system employs a Beckhoff PLC as the core controller, responsible for longitudinal/traverse movement control, tying operations, and executing commands from the upper computer. The electrical architecture utilizes a 48V lithium battery as the main power source, supplying critical components like sensors, motors, and relays through multi-stage voltage conversion modules. Experiments conducted on a 4000mm ×4000mm rebar mesh (containing 400 nodes) showed that the robot ‘s average longitudinal movement error was 4.37mm, the average traverse movement error was 3.4mm, and the node tying success rate reached 95.3%. The experimental results validate the system ‘s precision and engineering reliability.

abstract_ts: We present a low-cost bionic butterfly flight control system based on an embedded controller and multi-sensor fusion. This system integrates the controller, multi-sensor fusion, and intelligent algorithms to achieve real-time attitude control and environmental perception. Flapping motion is generated via symmetric servo linkages, while Kalman filtering enhances flight stability. The modular architecture facilitates closed-loop control and human-computer interaction. This design is suitable for educational applications and lightweight sensing tasks. Furthermore, it provides valuable insights for the future development of intelligent bionic unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) and military reconnaissance platforms.

abstract_ts: Workarounds enable users to achieve goals despite system limitations but expose design flaws, reduce productivity, risk compromising data quality, and cause inconsistencies. This study investigates how users employ workarounds when the data they want to enter does not align with software form constraints. Through a descriptive user study, we analyzed how workarounds originate and impact system design and data integrity. Understanding workarounds is essential for software designers to identify unmet user needs.

abstract_ts: The introduction to the special issue starts with a literature review with particular focus on distinguishing between the key terms and concepts of burnout, compassion and empathy fatigue. By looking at past studies, the author notes that vicarious trauma, secondary trauma, indirect trauma and secondary traumatic stress disorder are often used by working professionals and interchangeably; however the use of the concepts in the field-based social science research needs methodological adjustments and theoretical reconsideration. In the second part, she reflects on the evidence collected during the workshop ‘The Cost of Bearing Witness: Secondary Trauma and Self-Care in Field-Based Social Research’ which was held at the University of Turku, Finland, in October 2018. During the workshop participating researchers and practitioners discussed some of the most striking obstacles and challenges of the research work in the traumatized settings. On the basis of the ideas collected and shared, the authors synthetize some concrete suggestions for preventive and self-care techniques and ways to enhance overall the institutional and systematic support available to researchers exposed to trauma.

abstract_ts: This study investigates the application of the Hu-man Evolutionary Optimization Algorithm (HEOA), a recently developed bio-inspired metaheuristic for tuning a Proportional-Integral-Derivative controller with a filtered derivative term (PIDN) in the Load Frequency Control (LFC) of renewable-integrated power systems. The proposed HEOA-tuned controller is evaluated under diverse operating configurations,accounting for shifts in load and the integration of clean energy systems such as PV and wind turbines. Comparative analysis with a Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-based PIDN controller demonstrates that the HEOA achieves superior dynamic performance, charac-terized by reduced overshoot and improved transient response. Simulation results confirm the robustness and efficacy of HEOA as a reliable tuning strategy for modern LFC applications.

abstract_ts: Batteries are participated in many power system networks and our daily life. Charging the battery banks through Photovoltaic (PV) systems is an interesting research topic due to its numerous applications. Usually a bidirectional flow of power must be facilitate between the network and the battery bank. Hence a bidirectional device is facilitated between the dc-link and battery where dc-link is connected by PV system and load point. The PV system is consisting of multiple PV modules, hence, there is a chance to receive various solar irradiances by different modules. Hence, it will lead to the partial shading condition (PSC) where PV system can exhibits several maximum power points (MPPs). Under this operation, traditional maximum power point tracking (MPPT) techniques are unable to track exact power related to global maximum power location. Therefore, it is essential to integrate an effective optimization technique with traditional MPPT algorithms to boost system efficiency and improve overall system performance. A black winged kite algorithm (BWKA) is proposed in this research work to identify the location of global MPP during PSC of the PV system. The performance of the presented technique is compared with modified invasive weed optimization (MIWO), modified grey wolf optimization (MGWO) and whale optimization algorithm (WOA). Further, the control of bidirectional device of the battery is integrated with proposed BWKA method to work the same converter as a MPPT device of the PV system. Hence, this configuration can avoid an extra converter for the purpose of MPPT device. The system is designed using the Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) methodology on the OPAL-RT platform to showcase a variety of results.

abstract_ts: This study investigates and compares the performance of a conventional 8 by 8 split ring resonator microstrip patch antenna and a novel 8 by 8 split ring resonator-based fractal array antenna, both designed to operate within the S band frequency range of 2 to 3 gigahertz with a 50-ohm characteristic impedance. A total of 80 antenna samples were analyzed, 40 in each group, using return loss and standing wave ratio as the primary performance metrics. Statistical evaluation was conducted using a predefined alpha level of 0.05, beta level of 0.2, and a test power of 80 percent. The simulation results showed that the fractal-based antenna significantly outperformed the conventional design. The novel antenna achieved a return loss of minus 30.2889 decibels and a standing wave ratio of 1.0631, compared to minus 19.1511 decibels and 1.2479 for the standard configuration. The performance differences were statistically significant with p values less than 0.005. These findings indicate that the 8 by 8 fractal array antenna provides better impedance matching and lower signal reflection, making it more suitable for S band wireless communication applications.

abstract_ts: A Differential Difference Transconductance Amplifier (DDTA) based fractional-order tunable multifunction filter circuit using continued fraction expansion (CFE) is employed in the proposed design. The filter structure utilizes two Differential Difference Transconductance Amplifiers (DDTAs) and a single grounded fractional capacitor, leading to a versatile filter design with four inputs and a single output that supports low, high, and all-pass filter responses at fractional orders in inverting as well as in non-inverting modes in a single topology. The proposed configuration allows for electronic tunability, with the filter characteristics being adjustable by modifying the biasing current (I<inf>b</inf>) and the fractional order (α). The CFE approximation is used to get the rational function for the fractional-order Laplacian operator, which is realized by Foster-I and Foster-II topologies using MATLAB. Furthermore, the circuit's performance is validated using LT SPICE software using a 180-nm CMOS technology.

abstract_ts: In order to improve the accuracy and stability of air traffic flow prediction, an end-to-end deep learning-based model is proposed in this paper. By analyzing spatial correlations of adjacent areas and temporal correlations of historical traffic on given area, we firstly apply the gridded map method (including the flight levels) to encode the whole air traffic flow situation into a new data representation, i.e., traffic flow matrix (TFM). By the proposed data representation, inherent features of air traffic flow and their transition patterns on different cells and flight levels can be represented comprehensively. Learning from the powerful ability of convolutional neural network and recurrent neural network on modeling spatial and temporal correlations, the ConvLSTM module is proposed to build a trainable model for air traffic flow prediction. Since the output of the proposed model is also the TFM and shows the overall air traffic situation of studied regions, we call the proposed model as an end-to-end one. Experimental results on real data show the superior performance over existing approaches on prediction accuracy and stability. Furthermore, the proposed model can also predict the flow distribution on different flight levels in our application, which promotes the level of air traffic management. By analyzing the distribution of prediction errors on different cells, flight levels and predicting instants, we can draw the conclusion that spatial and temporal transition patterns of flight flow in air traffic system are fully learned by the proposed model. With the proposed model, more efficient air traffic flow measures are expected to be fulfilled to improve the operation efficiency..

abstract_ts: Entrepreneurship in rural areas greatly assists in increasing economic equality and lowering differences between regions. It is challenging to accurately foresee if rural entrepreneurial ventures will do well, because many different factors influence socio-economic and business behaviours. For this research, we introduce a new hybrid model that uses both Gradient Boosting (XGBoost, CatBoost) and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) as a classifier to improve accuracy in the study of rural entrepreneurship. The strategy included gathering diverse datasets with socio-demographic, financial and business data by using government databases and carrying out field surveys. Once normalization, feature engineering and balancing classes using SMOTE were performed, various Gradient Boosting models were fitted to obtain meta-features as probabilities. They were next given to an SVM classifier using the stacking strategy, alongside 10-fold cross-validation for making the model widely applicable. Experiments show that the hybrid model performs much better than standard classifiers. It reached a classification accuracy of 96.34%, a precision of 91.24%, a recall of 90.01%, an F1-score of 90.59% and an AUC-ROC score of 96.55% which is higher than XGBoost, LightGBM and Logistic Regression. Since the model outperformed others, it is proven to capture the unique factors that shape rural business achievements. The framework can help policymakers, development agencies and financial institutions direct their support more effectively. The study points out that machine learning models that use multiple algorithms can help handle rural economy and society issues by processing data effectively.

abstract_ts: In recent years, the increasing demand for sustainable and energy-efficient solutions has led to the development of smart technologies in various sectors. A smart solar street light system equipped with dynamic brightness control and fault detection mechanisms can enhance the efficiency and reliability of outdoor lighting infrastructure. The system utilises solar energy as a renewable power source, reducing dependence on conventional electricity grids and minimizing the environmental impact. The system employs PIR sensors to detect the motion of vehicles or pedestrians, automatically adjusting the brightness of the street lights in response. This dynamic brightness control not only ensures optimal visibility but also maximizes energy savings by dimming the lights during periods of low activity. To enhance the system's reliability, a comprehensive fault detection mechanism has been integrated. The fault detection system monitors the functioning of lights through LDR modules. In the event of a malfunction or abnormality, the system is designed to promptly identify the issue and trigger appropriate alerts. The street light control system is configured to integrate with the Blynk platform. This integration allows the system to communicate with the Blynk servers and utilize its features, including sending notifications to connected devices via the Blynk app. This feature facilitates proactive maintenance, minimizing downtime and ensuring uninterrupted illumination of the street. A web interface is implemented, serving as a centralized platform for remote monitoring, control, and management of the street light system, while providing real-time insights. The integration of smart technologies not only makes the system an energy-efficient and environmentally friendly solution but also contributes to the creation of intelligent urban lighting systems. As cities continue to evolve towards smart and sustainable environments, the implementation of such innovative systems becomes imperative for a greener and more efficient future.

abstract_ts: Understanding individual genetic factors has led to significant improvements in disease prediction, diagnosis and tailored treatment, and data well beyond what the human mind can comprehend can only be managed through machine learning (ML). It focuses on the use of ML techniques for analyzing and interpreting genomic data, particularly in their contributions to the identification of disease-associated genetic variants. ML uses one or a combination of supervised and unsupervised learning models, deep learning architectures, and ensemble methods to guide the extraction of meaningful information from complex genetic data. The feature selection techniques, including recursive feature elimination (RFE) and principal component analysis (PCA), improve model performance by minimizing dimensions and removing noise. Specific disease susceptibility can be predicted with high accuracy from the genetic markers using classification algorithms, such as random forests (RF), support vector machines (SVM), and neural networks. Clustering techniques (k-means, hierarchical clustering, etc.) are also used to identify hidden genetic subgroups associated with particular diseases. With the development of next-generation sequencing (NGS) and genome-wide association studies (GWAS), big data sets have emerged encouraging the application of ML-based approaches for instance to allow fast and accurate variant classification. CNNs (convolutional neural networks) are effective in modelling sequence-based data, while RNNs (recurrent neural networks) have demonstrated utility in recognizing pathogenic mutations. Naturallanguage processing (NLP) is another approach that further improves the variant annotation process by extracting valuable information about variants from biomedical publications. Future Work In this paper reinforcement learning in used for genetic diagnosis and treatment planning. Explainable AI methods make sure that the model is transparent and reliable and mitigate the black-box issue of deep learning models. However, challenges remain such as data heterogeneity, class imbalance, and explaining ML models. Some federated learning frameworks can aid genome privacy-preserving analysis by allowing collaborative model training without the aggregation of sensitive patient data. These approaches come with ethical questions including potential biases in genetic information resources and fair access to ML solutions in healthcare, that need to be constantly monitored. Further research could aim to improve model generalizability across different populations and develop hybrid ML methods that utilize both statistical and deep learning approaches to provide optimal predictive accuracy. This impressive potential for ML to revolutionize precision medicine lies in its ability to achieve disease prediction, targeted therapeutics development, and hence combat diseases at branch level.

abstract_ts: The increasing demand for cost-effective in-line inspection methods in the food industry necessitates the development of non-invasive techniques capable of detecting small foreign bodies that may contaminate products during production. A novel approach for a microwave imaging system, tailored for the inspection of a wide variety of food products, is proposed. The system employs CST Microwave Studio to simulate the virtual model of the food product, ensuring high spatial resolution suitable for different food types. The image reconstruction is done with the help of MATLAB. Filters are utilized to reduce the noise in the images. Furthermore, various techniques are used to enhance the quality of the obtained image to obtain accurate results. The defects are detected by using thresholding techniques and isolating the region of interest. The microwave imaging strategies used leverage the inherent symmetries of food items. Additionally, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are also used to predict the position of the anomaly. Experimental characterization of the system demonstrates results that align favourably, validating overall efficacy and confirming its potential as a viable solution for food product inspection.

abstract_ts: The effects of using copper metal foam as a volumetric absorber as well as heat transfer enhancement technique on the thermal characteristics of a direct absorption parabolic trough solar collector are examined experimentally. The thermal performance, temperature difference and friction factor for PTC in three cases (full porous, semi-porous and without porous) are investigated and compared with each other. The experiments were performed in different flow rates and inlet temperatures. The thermal performance of solar collector is calculated based on ASHRAE Standard 93-2010. Obtained results indicate that using porous foam increase the friction factor dramatically. Moreover, the maximum temperature differences for full-porous, semi-porous and free porous are 12.2 °C, 8.8 °C and 3.3 °C, respectively. It is found that by increasing the flow rate and decreasing the inlet temperature, the thermal efficiency of the collector can be enhanced. In addition, the maximum thermal efficiency improvements due to using full metal foam and semi metal foam compared to that of free metal foam are 171.2% and 119.6%, respectively..

abstract_ts: We introduce Quantum Molecular Autoencoder (MolQAE), the first quantum autoencoder to leverage the complete molecular structures. MolQAE uniquely maps Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES) strings directly to quantum states using parameterized rotation gates, preserving vital structural information. Its quantum encoder-decoder framework enables latent space compression and reconstruction. A dual-objective strategy optimizes fidelity and minimizes trash state deviation. Our evaluations demonstrate effective capture of molecular characteristics and a remarkable preservation of fidelity, approaching robust molecular reconstruction even with substantial dimensionality reduction. Our model introduces a novel quantum pathway in cheminformatics, capable of processing complete molecular structural information with a dedicated quantum architecture considering the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ)-era development and promising significant advances in drug and materials discovery.

abstract_ts: Fluorescent chemosensors based on pillararene complexes represent a new, promising branch in sensor technology. Because of CH⋅⋅⋅π interactions, aliphatic chains are well suited for the columnar cavities of pillararenes and bulky or sheet-like (sub)structures can be arranged on the portals. Thus, pillararenes form versatile receptors and an alteration of the fluorescence behavior upon complexation ensures the function of these chemosensors as the reporter. Although this field of research exists only since a few years, remarkable chemosensors were developed for substances as diverse as medical drugs, biochemicals, herbicides and explosives..

abstract_ts: The general idea of the AMBER/Child Alert System is that by broadcasting and distributing information about a missing child to the community, the public's involvement can trigger critical feedback that would have otherwise been ignored. This feedback, in several cases, can be proved decisive in finding the missing child. Despite the efforts at country and European level to effectively address the issue of missing children, a number of key challenges remain open, including lack of location-focused distribution of alerts, insufficient capture and diffusion of information, and lack of a mechanism that uses and merges all available sources of information. The aim of this paper is to present a novel approach for handling such challenges through a data analytics platform and a mobile application available to all citizens. Using the active research fields of human mobility pattern analysis and machine learning, we show that missing children investigations, as well as search and rescue operations, can be actively supported and enhanced when multiple data sources are combined and analyzed.

abstract_ts: The capacity of Mycoplasmas to engage in horizontal gene transfers has recently been highlighted. Despite their small genome, some of these wall-less bacteria are able to exchange multiple, large portions of their chromosome via a conjugative mechanism that does not conform to canonical Hfr/oriT models. To understand the exact features underlying mycoplasma chromosomal transfer (MCT), extensive genomic analyses were performed at the nucleotide level, using individual mating progenies derived from our model organism, Mycoplasma agalactiae. Genome reconstruction showed that MCT resulted in the distributive transfer of multiple chromosomal DNA fragments and generated progenies composed of a variety of mosaic genomes, each being unique. Analyses of macro- and micro-events resulting from MCT revealed that the vast majority of the acquired fragments were unrelated and co-transferred independently from the selection marker, these resulted in up to 17% of the genome being exchanged. Housekeeping and accessory genes were equally affected by MCT, with up to 35 CDSs being gained or lost. This efficient HGT process also created a number of chimeric genes and genetic micro-variations that may impact gene regulation and/or expression. Our study unraveled the tremendous plasticity of M. agalactiae genome and point toward MCT as a major player in diversification and adaptation to changing environments, offering a significant advantage to this minimal pathogen.

abstract_ts: Background: In the modern era of interdisciplinary business environments, evaluating the quality of applied talent cultivation has become essential for organizations determined to maintain innovation, sustainable and adaptability evolution. Challenge: The limitation of the existing Fuzzy Optimized Talent Cultivation Evaluation (FOTCE) model has faced limitation with its context-insensitive fuzzy evaluation mechanism, which restricts adaptability with complex talent cultivation in modern business disciplines. Proposed Model: to overcome these limitations, this research proposes a Random Forest-Enhanced Context-Aware Fuzzy Evaluation (RF-CAFE) framework for applied talent cultivation in new business settings. Initially, data is collected from International Business Machines and Human Resources (IBM-HR) analytics dataset and preprocessed using zeroimputation, outlier removal, min-max normalization and onehot encoding. After that, the fuzzification process converts numerical indicators into linguistic variables and context-aware transformation adjusts them based on business environment attributes. Then, the RF algorithm learns the feature importance and integrates these as adaptive fuzzy weights for computing the final Talent Quality Score (TQS) through centroid defuzzification. Results: Experimental results demonstrates that the proposed RF-CAFE model in term of Employee Engagement Index (EEI) as 98% and Talent Cultivation Development Efficiency Score (TCDES) as 96%, when compared with existing FOTCE model.
